services.title: Curriculum Design & Review
services.description: We develop and adapt curricula that are competency-based, inclusive, culturally responsive, and aligned with both national and global standards. Our work supports both formal and non-formal education settings.

services.title: ICT Integration in Education
services.description: We support schools and institutions to embed Information and Communication Technology (ICT) into teaching and learning. This includes:
subItems: Training for teachers in educational technology tools
ICT integration into teaching and learning

services.title: Teacher Training & Professional Development
services.description: We design and deliver continuous professional development (CPD) programs that improve teaching practice, subject mastery, classroom management, and learner-centred pedagogy.

services.title: Stakeholder Capacity Development
services.description: We strengthen the capacity of education stakeholders—including school leaders, policy makers, and community actors—through training, coaching, and systems support. We help build collaborative and effective education ecosystems.

services.title: Research, Monitoring & Evaluation
services.description: We conduct educational research, needs assessments, and evaluations to inform policy, program design, and implementation.

services.title: Policy Advocacy & Transformation
services.description: We support governments and institutions to develop, review, and implement education policies that are inclusive, evidence-based, and aligned with national and global education goals.

approach.title: Participatory & Contextualized
approach.description: We co-design solutions with clients and communities to ensure relevance and sustainability.

about.title: About PDIA
about.subtitle: Advancing quality, relevance, and equity in learning.
about.whoWeAre: PDIA Ltd is a leading education-focused consultancy firm committed to advancing quality, relevance, and equity in learning. We specialize in education system strengthening through evidence-based solutions in curriculum design, teacher training, ICT integration, and capacity building for key education stakeholders. And advocacy for integration of evidence-based innovations into national policy frameworks. With a team of experienced educators, policy experts, curriculum specialists, digital learning experts and instruction and pedagogy experts, we partner with governments, NGOs, schools, and development organizations to transform education.
about.mission: To deliver innovative, inclusive, and sustainable education solutions that empower educators, learners, and communities to thrive in a dynamic and digital world.
about.vision: To be a trusted partner in redefining education through locally relevant, globally informed, and technology-enhanced solutions.

servicesSubtitle: Providing evidence-based solutions to strengthen education systems.

blog.title: Transforming Education Through Competency-Based Curricula
blog.excerpt: Competency-based education shifts the focus from memorisation to measurable skills. Here's how PDIA is driving this shift across Uganda.
blog.content:
Competency-based education (CBE) is reshaping how students learn and how teachers teach. Unlike traditional models, CBE prioritises what a learner can do over what they have been exposed to. At PDIA, we have partnered with schools and ministries to design curricula that embed 21st-century competencies — critical thinking, collaboration, digital literacy, and problem solving — into every subject area.

Our approach begins with a thorough needs assessment, working alongside teachers, administrators, and community stakeholders to understand the local context. We then design learning outcomes that are measurable, relevant, and progressive. The result is a curriculum that prepares learners not just for exams, but for life.
blog.author: PDIA Editorial Team

blog.title: The Role of ICT in Bridging Uganda's Education Gap
blog.excerpt: Technology is no longer a luxury in education — it is a necessity. PDIA explores how ICT can close the gap between urban and rural learning outcomes.
blog.content:
Access to quality education remains unequal across Uganda, with rural learners often receiving fewer resources and less experienced teachers. ICT integration offers a powerful equaliser. When implemented thoughtfully, digital tools can deliver engaging content, support remote professional development for teachers, and provide learners with access to a world of knowledge.

PDIA's ICT integration programmes focus on training both teachers and administrators, ensuring technology is embedded in pedagogy — not merely placed in classrooms. We work to build sustainable ICT ecosystems that communities can maintain and grow.
blog.author: PDIA Editorial Team
